title: First-Stop vs Later-Stop Decomposition
summary: The strategic logic for the opening stop differs from later stops: the first stop is constrained by start compound, traffic, and opening stint degradation, while later stops are closer to endgame window management.
rationale: The strategic logic for the opening stop differs from later stops: the first stop is constrained by start compound, traffic, and opening stint degradation, while later stops are closer to endgame window management. Public F1 pit-decision work also reports gains from multi-task horizon-aware setups rather than one undifferentiated binary head.
implementation_hint: Create a `PitCountSoFar` or `IsFirstStintStopCandidate` feature from within-race driver history, then train separate heads/models for `pit count so far == 0` and `>=1`. Optionally add an auxiliary regression target for laps-until-next-stop only within each branch, using its OOF prediction as a feature for the binary classifier.
expected_effect: Cleaner decision boundaries and better calibration around early-race versus end-of-race pit logic.
risk: Branching reduces sample size; if split too finely, later-stop specialists may become unstable on rare wet or long-race regimes.
